one_line_value_proposition: Dietitian-supported digital weight management with NICE HTG700 recommendation — a scalable tier 2/3 service model relevant to NHS weight management transformation.
why_it_matters_locally: Oviva combines app-based food tracking, goal-setting and personalised dietitian support to deliver weight management at scale. It is particularly relevant to commissioners building pathways for patients on anti-obesity medications (GLP-1 agonists) who need structured behavioural support.
target_problem_statement: Limited NHS tier 2/3 weight management capacity, rising anti-obesity medication prescribing without adequate behavioural support, and inequitable access to dietitian services.
evidence_summary: NICE HTG700 recommendation. Multiple peer-reviewed studies supporting app-delivered dietitian-supported weight management. Evidence includes RCTs and real-world studies across NHS and international settings.
live_sites: Multiple NHS organisations across England and internationally
patients_covered_note: Tens of thousands of patients in NHS weight management pathways. Growing deployment linked to NHS anti-obesity medication pathways.
dtac_note: Confirm current DTAC status with Oviva before commissioning.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with Oviva.
target_patients: Adults with overweight or obesity referred to tier 2 or tier 3 weight management services.
exclusions: Confirm clinical exclusion criteria with Oviva and local pathway leads.
onboarding_detail: Clinician referral into structured programme. Patient accesses app with assigned dietitian support.
supplier_wrap: Oviva provides dietitian staffing, programme management and outcome reporting.
local_wraparound_detail: Referral pathway and patient identification process required. May need integration with local tier 3 or medication pathway.
training_note: Referrer training and pathway agreement required.
monitoring_note: Non-continuous dietitian review. Not a continuously monitored service.
escalation_note: Oviva dietitians provide support within programme. Escalation to tier 3 or clinical services as clinically indicated.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. Ensure patients have appropriate escalation routes.
implementation_prerequisites: Patient identification and eligibility criteria agreed
indicative_price_text: Contact Oviva for NHS pricing. Bundled service model includes dietitian support.
service_wrap_note: Dietitian support is part of the Oviva service model.
contract_note: Contact Oviva for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: Evidence of clinically meaningful weight loss and behaviour change in NHS tier 2/3 populations. Relevant to anti-obesity medication prescribing pathway support.
tariff_considerations: Bundled service model — compare with face-to-face tier 2/3 service costs. Consider GLP-1 medication prescribing pathway integration.
roi_note: Weight management ROI models should include reduced comorbidity burden and medication costs over time.
nhse_125k_note: Not applicable — this is a weight management product.
confidence_note: Content based on publicly available information. Verify assurance and pricing with Oviva before procurement.

one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG700-recommended digital weight management with health coach support — a scalable, lower-intensity digital tier 2 alternative.
why_it_matters_locally: Second Nature offers a health coach-supported digital programme that can serve as a lower-intensity complement to dietitian-led services, broadening access to weight management support.
target_problem_statement: Limited tier 2 weight management capacity and long waiting times for face-to-face services.
evidence_summary: NICE HTG700 recommendation. Published evidence on digital health coach-supported weight management.
patients_covered_note: Large NHS and consumer user base.
dtac_note: Confirm current DTAC status with Second Nature.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with Second Nature.
target_patients: Adults with overweight or obesity suitable for tier 2 digital weight management.
exclusions: Confirm clinical exclusion criteria with Second Nature.
onboarding_detail: Self-referral or clinician referral. App-based onboarding with health coach assignment.
local_wraparound_detail: Low local effort. Referral pathway or self-referral access required.
monitoring_note: Guided self-help — health coach support but not continuous clinical monitoring.
escalation_note: Escalation to clinical services as appropriate.
implementation_prerequisites: Referral pathway or self-referral process established
indicative_price_text: Contact Second Nature for NHS pricing.
contract_note: Contact Second Nature for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: Clinically meaningful weight loss with health coach support at scale.
tariff_considerations: Compare with face-to-face tier 2 service costs.
roi_note: Lower intensity than dietitian-led services — may be more cost-effective at tier 2.
demo_notes: Contact Second Nature for a demonstration.
confidence_note: Verify current assurance and pricing with Second Nature before procurement.

one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG766-recommended digital MSK self-management with strong commissioner relevance and broad NHS adoption across musculoskeletal and osteoarthritis pathways.
why_it_matters_locally: MSK conditions account for a significant proportion of GP appointments and physiotherapy referrals. getUBetter gives patients evidence-based guided self-management for hip and knee osteoarthritis, reducing pressure on first-contact physiotherapy and orthopaedic waiting lists.
target_problem_statement: High MSK demand on GP and physiotherapy services, long orthopaedic waiting lists, and inadequate support for patients between appointments.
evidence_summary: NICE HTG766 recommendation. Real-world evidence from multiple NHS deployments showing symptom improvement and reduced GP/physio appointments.
live_sites: Multiple NHS trusts and ICBs across England
patients_covered_note: Large NHS deployment base. Widely adopted across MSK first-contact physiotherapy pathways.
dtac_note: Confirm current DTAC status with getUBetter.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with getUBetter.
target_patients: Adults with mild to moderate symptoms of hip or knee osteoarthritis. Also covers ankle, back, elbow, foot, hand, knee, neck and shoulder conditions.
exclusions: Not indicated for patients requiring urgent clinical assessment or where red flag symptoms are present.
onboarding_detail: Self-referral or first-contact physiotherapist referral. Condition-specific pathway selected on registration.
supplier_wrap: getUBetter provides digital programme, analytics dashboard and population outcome reporting.
local_wraparound_detail: Low local effort. Referral pathway and clinician awareness required. Analytics dashboard available for population monitoring.
monitoring_note: Guided self-management — not a monitored clinical service. Patients are supported to self-manage with structured exercises and advice.
escalation_note: App includes safety screening and red flag signposting. Escalation to clinical services as clinically indicated.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored clinical service. Red flag symptoms require clinical escalation.
service_wrap_note: Analytics dashboard and implementation support included.
contract_note: Contact getUBetter for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: Evidence of symptom improvement and reduced MSK demand on NHS services.
tariff_considerations: Model using GP appointment and physiotherapy referral costs. Reduction in orthopaedic outpatient referrals is a relevant commissioner outcome.
roi_note: Reduction in physiotherapy and GP appointments is the primary ROI driver.
confidence_note: Verify current assurance and pricing with getUBetter before procurement.

one_line_value_proposition: NICE HTG768-recommended digital self-help for bulimia nervosa — an important exemplar of conditional digital use with robust pathway controls and clinical safety framing.
why_it_matters_locally: Eating disorder services face significant capacity pressures. Overcoming Bulimia Online offers a structured digital CBT-based self-help programme that can be deployed within clear clinical boundaries for appropriate patients — reducing waiting times and expanding access.
target_problem_statement: Long eating disorder service waiting times, limited access to evidence-based CBT for bulimia, and pressure on specialist CAMHS/adult eating disorder services.

nice_guidance_refs.note: Recommended during evidence generation for specific presentations

evidence_summary: NICE HTG768 recommendation during evidence generation. CBT-based self-help with established evidence base for bulimia nervosa. Pathway controls and clinical inclusion/exclusion criteria are essential — NICE guidance specifies conditions of use.
patients_covered_note: Limited NHS deployment at present. Evidence generation in progress.
dtac_note: Confirm current DTAC status with Overcoming.
gdpr_note: Confirm data hosting and DPA terms with Overcoming.
target_patients: Adults with bulimia nervosa meeting NICE HTG768 eligibility criteria. Clinical assessment required before referral.
exclusions: NICE guidance specifies strict inclusion/exclusion criteria. Not appropriate for patients with severe eating disorders, high medical risk, or those requiring intensive specialist input. Clinical safety review essential before deployment.
onboarding_detail: Clinician-led assessment and referral. Clinical safety review and pathway agreement required before deployment.
supplier_wrap: Overcoming provides the digital programme and reporting.
local_wraparound_detail: Robust clinical pathway controls essential. Clinical safety review, local inclusion/exclusion criteria and escalation protocols must be established before deployment. This is not suitable for unstructured self-referral.
training_note: Clinical training on inclusion/exclusion criteria and safety monitoring essential.
monitoring_note: Guided self-help — not continuously monitored. Active clinical oversight of patient progress required within service pathway.
escalation_note: Clear escalation to specialist eating disorder services essential. Patients must have access to clinical support.
operating_hours_caveat: Not a monitored emergency service. Robust escalation pathways to eating disorder services are mandatory before deployment.
implementation_prerequisites: Escalation pathway to specialist eating disorder services agreed
service_wrap_note: Clinical pathway wraparound is commissioner/provider responsibility.
contract_note: Contact Overcoming for NHS procurement options.
expected_benefit_note: Evidence of symptom reduction for bulimia nervosa with appropriate patient selection and pathway controls.
tariff_considerations: Model using eating disorder outpatient appointment costs and estimated reduction in service demand for appropriate patients.
roi_note: ROI dependent on patient selection and pathway design. Clinical safety must be the primary consideration.
confidence_note: Verify current assurance and pricing with Overcoming before procurement. Clinical safety review is mandatory before deployment.
clinical_safety_alert: This product has strict clinical inclusion/exclusion criteria and must only be deployed within a carefully designed pathway. Do not deploy without clinical safety review and specialist eating disorder service involvement.
